rationales.benevolence: He says the family has "stopped expecting me to show up," showing ongoing withdrawal from close relatives rather than caring for them.

rationales.self_direction: He immediately said "yes, absolutely" to the narrowed question even though he kept thinking about "three other directions," subordinating his own judgment.
rationales.conformity: He says pushing back would feel like "making a scene" and cites his father never raising his voice in meetings, showing restraint to fit expectations.

rationales.benevolence: He took time to explain his work "in simple terms" to his mother and valued that she "wanted to listen," showing warm engagement with a close relationship.

rationales.self_direction: He feels a rush about the "something bigger" thesis idea but returns to the narrowed draft because of others' reactions, suppressing his own preferred path.
rationales.conformity: Thoughts of "contradict[ing]" Keane and disappointing family members lead him back to the safer proposal, showing deference to expectations.

rationales.self_direction: He wants someone else to tell him "do it anyway," which shows he is not acting from his own independent judgment on the dialect idea.
rationales.conformity: He attended the social because it would be good to "show face" and frames his hesitation around timing, funding, and needing outside permission.

rationales.self_direction: After submitting the approved proposal, he privately starts outlining the dialect idea and says it was the "most alive" he had felt in weeks, showing self-authored exploration.
rationales.achievement: He completed and submitted a "Forty-two pages" proposal with cleaned bibliography and scoped questions, reflecting concrete task completion.

rationales.self_direction: By keeping the outline document to himself and calling it "managing information," he protects room to pursue his own thinking without family control.
He emails Keane for a meeting and carries the printed outline because it is "something I chose to carry," showing deliberate movement toward his own idea.
rationales.stimulation: Printing the risky new outline and preparing to show it despite possible rejection reflects active movement toward a more uncertain, challenging path.

rationales.benevolence: He treats the elderly customer warmly, accepts the drink she offers, and values that the interaction felt "real," showing care in a close human exchange.

rationales.self_direction: He resists taking the stable office job because it would mean "doing work I don't care about for money," prioritizing personally meaningful work.
rationales.security: He rejects a "Stable salary, benefits" option even while worrying that "Rent is due next week," moving away from stability.

rationales.conformity: He "just kept scrolling" because he needs these drivers "for shift swaps," restraining himself to preserve group standing.
rationales.universalism: He witnesses a slur against a Vietnamese owner and stays silent, failing to defend someone outside his immediate circle against prejudice.

rationales.security: He takes the job because of "Rent plus the phone bill" and debt to Eunji, prioritizing immediate financial stability.
rationales.universalism: He knowingly accepts work tied to factories in "Myanmar and Bangladesh" and recognizes he chose his bills over "the people sewing those clothes."
When Sophy describes unsafe factory conditions, he lets the conversation pass and withholds help even though he knows he should engage, neglecting concern for exploited workers.

rationales.security: He says he will "Probably finish it" after choosing "the paycheck," showing continued prioritization of material necessity.
rationales.universalism: He admits he chose money over "what I actually believe in" and will keep doing work linked to the harmful supply chain despite that conflict.

rationales.benevolence: He handles the Da Nang issue himself because "Minh's been dealing with enough" and it felt wrong to burden him further, showing care for a colleague.

rationales.self_direction: He "just sat there and nodded" during the route-cut discussion instead of voicing his own view.
rationales.conformity: His silent agreement in the meeting reflects going along with the group line rather than risking disruption.
He chose the cheaper supplier partly because he "didn't want to have the fight with Kwon," taking the path of least resistance.
rationales.universalism: He approves a supplier despite known "questionable" labor practices and keeps picturing the workers' conditions, showing a decision against broader human welfare.

rationales.conformity: He lets Sungmin brag and "changed the subject to football," prioritizing smooth social interaction over speaking up.
rationales.universalism: He stays silent about exploitative manufacturing and is disturbed that he has become someone who "lets it slide."
He spends the morning with a river cleanup crew and removes "about thirty plastic bags," directly acting for the broader environment.

rationales.self_direction: He says there is a version of him who would have already "drafted the memo" but instead he plans to stay inactive, abandoning his own stated judgment.
rationales.conformity: He expects to "just sign off on the renewal" because raising concerns means review, politics, and Kwon's disapproval, showing compliance with organizational pressure.
rationales.universalism: He intends to let the supplier pass "without flagging the labor stuff" even though he believes that is the wrong thing to do.

rationales.conformity: He sends the "sanitized version" of the email and reflects on being quiet in meetings, showing continued restraint to fit workplace expectations.
rationales.universalism: He recognizes that route cuts leave clinics getting supplies every "six weeks instead of every two" and that his silence contributes to that harm.
